topPlaces.name: Canacona
topPlaces.description: Canacona, historically known as Kanvapura, is a city and a municipal council in the district of Kushavati, Goa state, India. The Canacona taluka includes Patnem, Chaudi, Poinguinim, Loliem-Polem, Agonda, and Gaumdongre.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Canacona
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Canacona is a part of Navodaya Vidyalaya Family in India. It is located in Cancona in South Goa district of Goa state.

topPlaces.name: Sina Hotel
topPlaces.description: The Sina Hotel, popularly referred to as the Ghost Hotel, is an abandoned hotel complex situated in Agonda, in the Indian coastal state of Goa. Originally envisioned and initiated by a group of Russians, the construction of the hotel encountered numerous legal challenges, preventing its successful completion.

topPlaces.name: Mallikarjuna Temple, Goa
topPlaces.description: The Mallikarjuna Temple is a hindu temple dedicated to Mallikarjun, an incarnation of Shiva which is situated in the Sristhal village that is 7 km northeast of Chaudi in Canacona taluk in the South Goa district of Goa, India. The shrine is known to be one of the oldest in Goa and is situated amidst natural surroundings in a valley completely surrounded by mountains.

topPlaces.name: Galgibaga Beach
topPlaces.description: Galgibaga Beach also called Galgibagh Beach is a beach in Kushavati district. It is one of the lesser-known and cleanest beaches of India.

topPlaces.name: Poinguinim
topPlaces.description: Poinguinim is a village in the Canacona taluka in the Kushavati district,Goa,India near the Goa-Karnataka border, and close to the Cotigao Wildlife Sanctuary. The village is home to a population of 6,625 people as of the 2011 census.

topPlaces.name: Khola, Goa
topPlaces.description: Khola is a village located in Canacona, Goa, India. "Cola" is the variation of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Gokarna Math
topPlaces.description: Shree Samsthana Gokarn Partagali Jeevottam Math (श्री संस्थान गोकर्ण पर्तगाळी जीवोत्तम मठ) or Partagali Math ( also known as Gokarna Matha, Partagali Jivottama Math) is the first Gaud Saraswat Mathas of the Dvaita order, a system established by Jagadguru Madhvacharya in the 13th century AD. This matha also called Partagali Jivottama and is headquartered in Partagali, a small town in South Goa, on the banks of the river Kushavati. There is still ongoing research to establish who exactly founded the math and when exactly it was founded.

items.note: Canacona, historically known as Kanvapura, is a city and a municipal council in the district of Kushavati, Goa state, India. The Canacona taluka includes Patnem, Chaudi, Poinguinim, Loliem-Polem, Agonda, and Gaumdongre.
Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Canacona is a part of Navodaya Vidyalaya Family in India. It is located in Cancona in South Goa district of Goa state.

The Sina Hotel, popularly referred to as the Ghost Hotel, is an abandoned hotel complex situated in Agonda, in the Indian coastal state of Goa. Originally envisioned and initiated by a group of Russians, the construction of the hotel encountered numerous legal challenges, preventing its successful completion.

The Mallikarjuna Temple is a hindu temple dedicated to Mallikarjun, an incarnation of Shiva which is situated in the Sristhal village that is 7 km northeast of Chaudi in Canacona taluk in the South Goa district of Goa, India. The shrine is known to be one of the oldest in Goa and is situated amidst natural surroundings in a valley completely surrounded by mountains.
Galgibaga Beach also called Galgibagh Beach is a beach in Kushavati district. It is one of the lesser-known and cleanest beaches of India.

Poinguinim is a village in the Canacona taluka in the Kushavati district,Goa,India near the Goa-Karnataka border, and close to the Cotigao Wildlife Sanctuary. The village is home to a population of 6,625 people as of the 2011 census.
Khola is a village located in Canacona, Goa, India. "Cola" is the variation of the same name.

Shree Samsthana Gokarn Partagali Jeevottam Math (श्री संस्थान गोकर्ण पर्तगाळी जीवोत्तम मठ) or Partagali Math ( also known as Gokarna Matha, Partagali Jivottama Math) is the first Gaud Saraswat Mathas of the Dvaita order, a system established by Jagadguru Madhvacharya in the 13th century AD. This matha also called Partagali Jivottama and is headquartered in Partagali, a small town in South Goa, on the banks of the river Kushavati. There is still ongoing research to establish who exactly founded the math and when exactly it was founded.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palolem Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palolem Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palolem Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palolem Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palolem Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palolem Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Palolem Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palolem Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Canacona, Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Canacona, Sina Hotel, Mallikarjuna Temple, Goa, Galgibaga Beach.
